channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Missile Attack Kills at Least 3 in West Bank
item.description: Several others were injured in the Iranian attack that was the first to kill Palestinians in the Israeli-occupied West Bank since the war began. Residents in Tehran and elsewhere in Iran describe lives gripped by fear and anger.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: A Lebanese Cafe on the Edge of Destruction
item.description: Near the center of Beirut, one image captures the aftermath of an Israeli strike.
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: How TrumpRx Drug Prices Compare With Those in Other Countries
item.description: The TrumpRx website claims to offer the best prices for medications. Here’s where Americans still pay more — and much more.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins
item.media:description: President Trump unveiled the TrumpRx website last month. Visitors to the site are greeted by a promise: “Find the world’s lowest prices on prescription drugs.”

item.title: He Lost a Leg for Russia. Then, He Says, His Country Betrayed Him.
item.description: Imprisoned for murder, Aleksandr Abbasov-Derskhan sought a new start in life and freedom by signing up to fight in Ukraine. But he says promised benefits proved illusory.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins
item.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times
item.media:description: Aleksandr Abbasov-Derskhan in the factory dormitory where he works, reattaching his prosthesis after disinfecting stitches on his amputated leg that reopened due to prolonged standing.

item.title: For U.S., Unmet Expectations in Iran Fit a Familiar Pattern in the Region
item.description: Iran’s military retaliation, along with the political defiance of its new leaders, evokes a decades-old pattern of unrealized goals for American interventions in the region.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Iranian flag wrapping a building that was damaged in an airstrike in Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: Pakistan Pauses Afghanistan Airstrikes for Eid
item.description: At least 143 people were killed in a Pakistani airstrike that hit a drug rehabilitation facility in Kabul on Monday, according to a top U.N. official.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Safiullah Padshah and Zia ur-Rehman
item.media:description: A mass burial was held on Wednesday for people who died on Monday in a Pakistani airstrike on a drug rehabilitation center in Kabul, Afghanistan.

item.title: The Faroe Islands Are Changing Some of Europe’s Strictest Abortion Rules
item.description: A new law allowing abortion up to 12 weeks will be a major shift in an archipelago of 55,000 people, and there are strong feelings on both sides.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Regin Winther Poulsen
item.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Marna Jacobsen, 71, in Torshavn, Faroe Islands, late last year. Ms. Jacobsen said that as a 17-year-old she had to lie to get an abortion.

item.title: The Tropical St. Patrick’s Day That Honors African History
item.description: Montserrat treats the holiday as both a national celebration and a more somber milestone: a commemoration of a failed slave rebellion.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner and Luis Antonio Rojas

item.title: Retired Belgian Diplomat, 93, Faces Trial Over 1961 Killing of Congolese Leader
item.description: A Brussels court ruled that Étienne Davignon must stand trial for the murder of Patrice Lumumba, the first prime minister of the Democratic Republic of Congo.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:description: Patrice Lumumba, an independence leader who was the first Congolese prime minister, under detention in December 1960.

item.title: Netanyahu Hopes Strikes on Iran Will Lead to Uprising and Regime Change
item.description: Israeli attacks have targeted the command centers of Iran’s repressive, internal security forces in hopes that Iranians will overthrow their rulers. Some see that as wishful thinking.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Ronen Bergman and David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A new billboard in Tehran shows Iran’s three supreme leaders.

item.title: As a Meningitis Outbreak Spreads in Kent, UK, Here’s What to Know
item.description: At least 20 young adults are believed to be infected, and two have died, in an outbreak of meningococcal disease in the county of Kent, in southeast England.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.category: London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine
item.media:description: Students and faculty members wait in line for antibiotics at the University of Kent in Canterbury, England, on Monday.

item.title: An Abandoned Russian Tanker Is Drifting at Sea. Will Anyone Stop It?
item.description: An attack forced its crew to abandon ship in early March. No country has intervened, despite fears of environmental disaster.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.media:credit: Miguela Xuereb/Newsbook Malta, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The Arctic Metagaz, a Russian fuel tanker, adrift between Malta and Lampedusa, Italy, on Sunday.

item.title: Despite Moscow’s Threats, Poland Rules to Extradite Archaeologist
item.description: Kyiv accuses Alexander Butyagin, a prominent antiquities scholar, of destroying cultural heritage during his excavations in Russia-occupied Crimea. The Kremlin has condemned his arrest.
item.dc:creator: Nataliya Vasilyeva
item.media:description: Alexander Butyagin at a court in Warsaw in January. He was detained in Poland on a Ukrainian arrest warrant in December.

item.title: As Iran War Drags On, Europe Wants to Avoid a New Migration Crisis
item.description: A decade ago, a surge in migration to Europe spurred the far-right’s rise. European leaders now fear the Iran war could set off another crisis, and they have taken tentative steps to prepare.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley, Jeanna Smialek and Ben Hubbard
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Migrants in Slovenia at the height of the European migration crisis in 2015.

item.title: E.U. Loan for Ukraine Becomes Election Leverage for Orban
item.description: European leaders are pushing Prime Minister Viktor Orban to stop blocking a €90 billion loan to Ukraine. But the issue is a rallying cry in remarkably close elections in Hungary set for April 12.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes, Jeanna Smialek and Cassandra Vinograd
item.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary in Budapest on Sunday. His disputes with President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine have become a focal point of Hungary’s coming election.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado, Ravi Mattu, Rebecca Elliott and River Akira Davis

item.title: Oil Prices Keep Climbing on Heightened Fears Over Energy Supplies
item.description: Attacks on major energy facilities in Iran and Qatar injected new uncertainty into the outlook for oil and gas prices.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: The Iran War’s Economic Threat to Europe and Asia
item.description: Inflation and its consequences for growth are a growing concern for countries where memories of the 2022 energy crisis are fresh.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson and River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: A fuel station in Dhaka, Bangladesh, last week.

item.title: In Swaths of Germany, the Far-Right AfD Is Part of the Fabric
item.description: The Alternative for Germany party, or AfD, is shunned in federal politics but is a regional force. This fall, it could win broader power for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley, Tatiana Firsova and Clay Risen
item.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Three AfD representatives — Christian Skotnik, left, Doreen Gärtner, center, and Enrico Schult — before a party meeting in Stavenhagen, Germany, in October.

item.title: Ozempic Is About to Go Generic in India, China and Canada
item.description: In India, China and several other nations, Novo Nordisk is on the verge of losing patent protection for its blockbuster weight loss drug, opening the door for cheaper competing versions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins, Meaghan Tobin, Eshe Nelson, Alex Travelli and Pragati K.B.
item.media:credit: Saumya Khandelwal for The New York Times

item.title: Iranian Missile Attack Leaves 3 Palestinians Dead in West Bank
item.description: Several others were injured in the strike, which hit a caravan being used as a hair salon in the town of Beit Awwa, according to Palestinian officials.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Fatima AbdulKarim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Palestinian security forces gathering at the scene where three Palestinian women were killed in an Iranian missile attack near Hebron in the Israeli-occupied West Bank on Wednesday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Wednesday
item.description: A day of strikes on energy facilities and testimony in Washington to members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ras Laffan Industrial City, Qatar, earlier this month. A state-owned Qatari energy company said that Ras Laffan Industrial City had sustained “extensive damage” from missile attacks.

item.title: Iran Turns Out for Funeral for Those Killed in Strikes
item.description: Some died in Iran, others on a sea far from home. They were honored together Wednesday at a procession in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Days of Anger and Fear for Iranians Living With Waves of Airstrikes
item.description: Residents in Tehran and elsewhere say the attacks come at all hours and rock the ground.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Apartments in a neighborhood of Tehran targeted by American-Israeli airstrikes this week.

item.title: 2 Charged With Surveillance for Iran on London’s Jews
item.description: The suspects are accused of gathering information last summer on targets linked to the city’s Jewish community.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:description: Metropolitan Police headquarters in London. This week’s prosecutions are the latest of several related to hostile activity in Britain backed by Iran.

item.title: Empowering Iran’s Hard-liners
item.description: U.S.-Israeli attacks helped ultraconservatives cement power. That could make ending the war tougher.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Palestinian Man Recounts Brutal Sexual Assault by Israeli Settlers
item.description: The man said his attackers stripped him naked, beat him and zip-tied his genitalia, an account corroborated by family members and a rights activist who were also beaten.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Fatima AbdulKarim
item.media:credit: Afif Amireh for The New York Times
item.media:description: Suhaib Abualkebash, with a bruised eye, in Khirbet Humsa, in the Israeli-occupied West Bank. He said he had been assaulted by Israeli settlers.

item.title: Strikes Deliver Another Blow to Iran’s Domestic Energy Sites
item.description: The attacks could worsen the severe electricity shortages that Iranians already face.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Christina Goldbaum and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Part of the South Pars gas field facility near Kangan, Iran, in 2014. The extent of the damage on Wednesday was not immediately clear.

item.title: Iran Maintains Near-Total Internet Blackout Amid U.S.-Israeli Strikes
item.description: As the war has stretched into its third week, the Iranian government has blocked internet access for most of its 92 million citizens.
item.dc:creator: Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A woman with her phone at a market in Tehran this month. An internet blackout has cut off the majority of Iranians from communicating with the outside world.

item.title: Why Trump Keeps Saying ‘Nuclear’
item.description: Our national security reporter David E. Sanger describes how President Trump is considering a risky commando operation at an Iranian nuclear site.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Coleman Lowndes, Joey Sendaydiego, Estelle Caswell, Jon Hazell, Rafaela Balster and Whitney Shefte

item.title: U.S. Intelligence Saw No Change in Iran’s Missile Capabilities Before War
item.description: On Wednesday, the director of national intelligence and C.I.A. director contradicted one of the justifications the Trump administration had given for its attacks on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Tulsi Gabbard, the director of national intelligence, testified before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Wednesday.

item.title: Iran Executes Swedish Citizen Accused of Spying
item.description: The case indicates that more than two weeks into the war, Iran continues its prosecution of foreigners. Sweden said its citizen did not get a fair trial.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Energy Facilities Attacked in Iran and Qatar, Sending Prices Soaring
item.description: The strikes on a gas field in Iran and an industrial complex in Qatar appeared to be some of the most significant attacks on energy sites since the U.S.-Israeli air war against Iran began.
item.dc:creator: Ravi Mattu and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Refineries at the South Pars gas field in 2019.

item.title: Couple Killed in Cluster Bomb Attack Near Tel Aviv
item.description: Neighbors told Israeli news media that their apartment building was hit soon after a siren sounded.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Damage from a missile strike in Ramat Gan, Israel, on Wednesday. Two people were killed in the suburb of Tel Aviv.

item.title: Israel Has Killed Esmaeil Khatib, Iran’s Intelligence Minister
item.description: The Israeli military said on Wednesday that it had killed Esmaeil Khatib in an airstrike. Iran confirmed the killing.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner and Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Esmaeil Khatib, the Iranian intelligence minister, during a ceremony in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: Over 200 Ukrainian Experts in Middle East to Help Fight Drones, Zelensky Says
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky said that 11 countries in the region have asked for Kyiv’s assistance. Russia has launched such drones against Ukraine for years.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine speaking to British lawmakers in London, on Tuesday.

item.title: Israel Intensifies Strikes on Beirut, Escalating Its Campaign Against Hezbollah
item.description: The Israeli military widened its attacks to the districts in the center of the Lebanese capital, destroying buildings, forcing residents to flee and killing at least 10.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum and Sarah Chaayto
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The aftermath of an airstrike in central Beirut, Lebanon, on Wednesday.

item.title: Betting on Ayatollah’s Ouster Ignites Ire Over Prediction Markets
item.description: Bettors are angry that Kalshi, citing a policy against death bets, did not pay on wagers. And with signs of trading on government secrets, some lawmakers want to restrict prediction markets.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Fatima AbdulKarim, Rebecca F. Elliott and Ephrat Livni

item.title: Trump’s Friendship With Japan’s Leader Takaichi Faces Test Over Iran
item.description: In Washington on Thursday, President Trump is expected to press Sanae Takaichi for military help in the Strait of Hormuz. But she faces constraints on what she can offer.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump and Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi of Japan aboard the U.S.S. George Washington at Yokosuka Port south of Yokohama, Japan, in October.

item.title: Asia Turns Back to Coal as War Chokes Off Natural Gas
item.description: Across Asia, a sharp drop in liquefied natural gas supplies is pushing major importers back toward coal, undermining L.N.G.’s long-held role as a stable energy anchor.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: Qatar Energy announced a shutdown of its liquefied natural gas production after Iranian attacks on energy installations.

item.title: This is what happened on March 17.
Cuban Americans Will Be Allowed to Own Businesses in Cuba, but Is That Enough to Rescue the Economy?
item.description: The Cuban government announced that Cubans living abroad can own and invest in businesses, but experts said they were disappointed that the measures didn’t go further.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles
item.media:description: People stand outside a store in Havana on Monday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Tuesday
item.description: The deaths of two top Iranian officials were announced on Tuesday, while President Trump once again lashed out at NATO allies who refused to aid the U.S. war effort against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani, center, registering his name as a candidate for presidential elections in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: Trump’s Next Decision in War: Whether to Retrieve Iran’s Nuclear Fuel
item.description: A mission to seize or destroy Iran’s nuclear material would be one of the riskiest military operations in modern American history.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Vantor, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A satellite image of the Pickaxe Mountain tunnel complex near the Natanz nuclear facility in central Iran last week. It is not certain where all of Iran’s nuclear material resides.

item.title: Kharg Island Is an Appealing Target for Trump, With High Risks
item.description: A U.S. attack or a move to seize control of Iran’s main oil export hub could cripple the country’s ability to profit from its natural resources. But it would also risk sending energy prices even higher.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski, Rebecca F. Elliott and Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Kharg Island oil facility off the coast of Iran in 2017.

item.title: Ali Larijani, a Top Iranian Politician and Emissary, Is Dead at 67
item.description: Killed in an Israeli attack, he was Iran’s leading national security official and a confidant of the former supreme leader. He led a brutal crackdown on protests this year.
item.dc:creator: Ravi Mattu
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani in Tehran in 2024. Ayatollah Ali Khamenei made Mr. Larijani the de facto leader of Iran in January, when nationwide protests engulfed the country.

item.title: An Unexploded Bomb Near the Colombia-Ecuador Border Leads to a Diplomatic Clash
item.description: The Times photographed an unexploded munition in southern Colombia, near the Ecuadorean border. A high-stakes feud between both countries quickly ensued.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní, John Ismay and Federico Rios
item.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: The remnants of a 500‑pound undetonated bomb dropped by the Ecuadorian army that fell on a coca crop in Colombia.

item.title: Is Latin America Abandoning Cuba?
item.description: President Trump is turning up the pressure on Cuba, but other Latin American countries, even those on the left, are staying quiet. We look at why.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Ali Larijani, Iran’s De Facto Political Leader, Killed by Israel
item.description: The killing, which Iran confirmed, could embolden Iranian hard-liners, as the U.S.-Israeli bombing campaign continues.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Ronen Bergman and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani, Iran’s top security official, in Tehran in 2024. He was killed in an overnight strike, the Israeli military said.

item.title: As Trump belittled European allies, the Irish leader pushed back, gently.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump hosted Micheál Martin, the taoiseach or prime minister of Ireland, at the White House on Tuesday,

item.title: Israel Keeps Killing Key Iranian Leaders. Will It Work?
item.description: Israeli officials trumpeted airstrikes that killed Iran’s de facto leader and the commanders of a militia notorious for violently suppressing protests. But “decapitation has its limits,” an analyst warns.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani, Iran’s top security official, in Tehran in 2024. The Israeli military announced it had killed him in an airstrike on Tuesday.

item.title: The Iran War Is Pushing Some Countries to Cut Back On Energy Use
item.description: As the conflict enters its third week, some nations are trying to reduce energy use, including a mandatory energy holiday in Sri Lanka.
item.dc:creator: Claire Brown
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A damaged oil facility in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates, on Saturday.

item.title: U.N. Says Attacks on Iran Set to Drive Hunger to Record Levels
item.description: Tens of millions more people around the world could face acute hunger as the war in the Middle East continues to disrupt shipping and drive up oil prices.
item.dc:creator: Nick Cumming-Bruce
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Volunteers prepared packages of food in the Paktia Province of Afghanistan on Monday.

item.title: It Was the Vatican’s ‘Trial of the Century.’ Now, It’s Going to Retrial.
item.description: Three years ago, the Vatican’s criminal court convicted a cardinal for the first time in history. An appeals court has ruled there were irregularities in his prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.media:description: Cardinal Giovanni Angelo Becciu at the Vatican in 2024.

item.title: China Ramps Up Scrutiny of Meta’s Acquisition of Manus
item.description: The country appears to be cracking down on people linked to the acquisition of Manus, a Singapore company with Chinese roots, as President Trump prepares to visit Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson, Meaghan Tobin, Paul Mozur and Eli Tan
item.media:description: Meta announced in December that it had bought Manus, a Singapore artificial intelligence start-up that has Chinese roots.

item.title: Antisemitic Attacks Bring Deeper Unease to Dutch Jews
item.description: Since a school and a synagogue were attacked over two days, an unsettling feeling has spread among Jews in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses
item.media:description: Police standing outside a Jewish school following an explosion in Amsterdam on Saturday.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Doesn’t Need Allies to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump has faced mounting criticism over the U.S.-Israeli assault on Iran from allies and even members of his own administration.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump spoke to reporters on Tuesday while meeting with Micheál Martin, the prime minister of Ireland, in the Oval Office.

item.title: ‘What Is That?’: Ice Cream Brand’s Smelly Train Station Ad Falls Flat
item.description: Magnum Ice Cream is scaling back a scented ad at King’s Cross station after commuters complained that the aroma was “sickly” and “artificial.”
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.media:description: Every few seconds, spritzes of chocolate aroma fill King’s Cross Station in London, part of a marketing campaign for Magnum Ice Cream.

item.title: Ali Larijani Killing Fuels Anxiety in Iran
item.description: The death of Iran’s top national security official has ignited concern about the country’s future.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ali Larijani was appointed as the head of the Supreme National Security Council by the slain former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: An ‘Unprecedented’ Outbreak of Meningitis Raises Alarm in Britain
item.description: Two people have died in an outbreak of meningococcal disease. Many cases were traced to a nightclub in Canterbury that is popular with students at the University of Kent.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai
item.media:description: Staff and students waiting in line to receive antibiotics at the University of Kent in Canterbury on Monday.
